# @overflow-aware-list/react

> React component that renders only the items that physically fit inside a container. Items that overflow are hidden and passed to a `renderMore` render prop so you can show a "+N" badge, ellipsis, or any custom indicator. Supports horizontal and vertical directions, and can slice from either end of the list.

- GitHub: https://github.com/rai-aashish/overflow-aware-list/tree/main/packages/react
- Live demo: https://rai-aashish.github.io/overflow-aware-list/react/

## Installation

```bash
npm install @overflow-aware-list/react
```

Peer dependency: `react >= 18`.

## Quick start

```tsx
import { OverflowList } from "@overflow-aware-list/react";

<OverflowList
  items={tags}
  renderItem={(tag, i) => <span key={i} className="tag">{tag.label}</span>}
  renderOverflow={(hidden) => <span className="badge">+{hidden.length}</span>}
/>
```

The container fills `width: 100%`. Items that don't fit are hidden; `hidden` in `renderMore` is the array of items that were cut.

## Props

```ts
type OverflowListProps<T> = {
  /** Array of items to display. */
  items: T[];

  /** Layout axis. Default: "horizontal". */
  direction?: "horizontal" | "vertical";

  /**
   * Which end to keep visible when overflow occurs.
   * "start" — show first N items, overflow indicator at end (default).
   * "end"   — show last N items, overflow indicator at start (useful for breadcrumbs).
   */
  keepFrom?: "start" | "end";

  /** Render a single visible item. Receives the item and its original index in `items`. */
  renderItem: (item: T, index: number) => ReactNode;

  /**
   * Render the overflow indicator. Receives the array of hidden items.
   * Also rendered (with an empty array) in the off-screen measurement layer
   * so its size is always accounted for.
   */
  renderOverflow: (hidden: T[]) => ReactNode;
  /**
   * HTML tag for the outer container element. Default: "div".
   * Generic — rest props and ref narrow to match the chosen tag.
   * @example as="nav"     // ref: HTMLElement, restProps: nav attrs
   * @example as="button"  // ref: HTMLButtonElement, restProps: button attrs
   */
  as?: As;
} & Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLElementTagNameMap[As]>, "children">;
// As defaults to "div". Rest props and ref narrow automatically when as is set.
// ref is forwarded — pass a ref object or callback ref and it merges with the internal controller ref.
```

## Usage examples

### Horizontal tag row with "+N" badge

```tsx
const tags = ["React", "TypeScript", "Vite", "Tailwind", "Zustand"];

<div style={{ width: "300px" }}>
  <OverflowList
    items={tags}
    renderItem={(tag) => <span className="tag">{tag}</span>}
    renderOverflow={(hidden) => <span className="badge">+{hidden.length}</span>}
  />
</div>
```

### Breadcrumb — show deepest path segments

```tsx
const crumbs = ["~", "projects", "overflow-aware-list", "packages", "core"];

<OverflowList
  items={crumbs}
  direction="horizontal"
  keepFrom="end"
  renderItem={(crumb, i) => (
    <span className="crumb">
      {i > 0 && <span>/</span>}
      {crumb}
    </span>
  )}
  renderOverflow={(hidden) => <span>/…{hidden.length}/</span>}
/>
```

### Vertical notification feed

```tsx
<div style={{ height: "160px" }}>
  <OverflowList
    items={notifications}
    direction="vertical"
    renderItem={(n) => <div className="notif">{n.text}</div>}
    renderOverflow={(hidden) => <div>+{hidden.length} more</div>}
  />
</div>
```

### Custom container tag with typed ref

```tsx
const navRef = useRef<HTMLElement>(null);

<OverflowList
  as="nav"
  ref={navRef}
  items={crumbs}
  renderItem={(c) => <span>{c}</span>}
  renderOverflow={(h) => <span>+{h.length}</span>}
/>
```

### Forwarded ref (plain div)

```tsx
const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

<OverflowList ref={ref} items={tags} renderItem={...} renderOverflow={...} />
```

### Hide content until measured (no layout flash)

```tsx
// renderItem receives the original index; use listState.measured if you need it.
// The component initialises with all items visible (SSR-safe), then trims on first paint.
// If you want to suppress the untrimmed flash, wrap with visibility:hidden until stable:
<div style={{ visibility: measured ? "visible" : "hidden" }}>
  <OverflowList ... />
</div>
```

## Styling

The component renders a `<{as} data-slot="overflow-list">` as the container (default `<div>`). It forces these styles (you cannot override them):

```
display: flex
align-items: start
justify-content: start
overflow: hidden
flex-direction: row | column   (from direction prop)
width: 100%                    (horizontal only)
height: 100%                   (vertical only)
```

All other styles and class names you pass are applied first, then the above override. Set `gap` via `className` or `style` — the component reads it from computed styles to account for it in size calculations.

Each item is wrapped in `<div data-overflow-item style="flex-shrink:0">`.

## How it works

1. Two render passes: visible container (clipped) + off-screen measurement layer (hidden, all items).
2. `ResizeObserver` watches the container, each item, and the "more" indicator.
3. On any size change, the controller computes how many items fit and calls `setState`.
4. React re-renders with the new `visibleItems` / `hiddenItems` split.

The off-screen layer is `position: absolute; visibility: hidden; left: -9999px` so it never affects layout.

## Implementation notes

- Controller is created once (`useEffect` with `[]`). Ref callbacks fire during commit (before `useEffect`) so element registration is deferred until the effect runs and then backfilled.
- `updateOptions` is called in a separate effect keyed on `[items, direction, sliceFrom]` — this keeps the initial effect stable and avoids re-creating the controller on prop changes.
- Item ref callbacks are memoised per-index in a `Map` to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
